steps.title: Close the appositive
steps.content: The dash after "radiant" opens an appositive definition: "the point in the sky from which the shower's meteors appear to emanate." That definition must close before the main clause continues with "is 39 degrees."

steps.title: Reject unmatched punctuation
steps.content: A closes the dash after the predicate, swallowing part of the main clause. C never closes the dash, and D tries to close it with a comma. The remaining choice closes the appositive after "emanate" and then continues the main clause correctly. The answer is B.
